School, Library & Book Club Presentations

School Visits & Contact Info

Kat enjoys speaking about her books and writing process, and she is comfortable doing presentations for groups of any size.

Presentations

Kat is willing to create a specific program to suit the needs of a conference, book club, library, or school. Her presentations are generally 45 minutes long, but can be tailored to fit the time available.

During her talk, Kat tries to get teen and tween readers excited about books and writing. She discusses how she comes up with story ideas and how much research goes into writing a science fiction novel. She also shares behind-the-scenes stories about her journey to publication.

Throughout the presentation, Kat shows projected images from her research that inspired and informed her middle grade Dark Life series and her young adult Inhuman series, including deep-sea creatures, future technology, and cutting edge science. These images always generate lots of questions from students and help keep the program lively and interactive. Afterwards, Kat is available for book signing and conversation.

Fees

Email Kat directly for information on honorarium fees:

katfalls77@gmail.com

AV Tech Needs

Kat has her PowerPoint presentation on a flash drive and therefore needs a computer attached to a projector and a screen. She also hopes for a good microphone if she is speaking to a large group.

Conferences & Book Festivals

Kat enjoys being on panels with other authors as well as giving solo presentations. She is also comfortable leading a workshop or breakout session on craft.

Skype Visits

Skype visits are always free. Send an email to katfalls77@gmail.com with a proposed date and time, and put SKYPE REQUEST in the subject line.

Ordering Books

You can order Kat's books from your nearby bookstore, or schools and libraries can order at a discounted rate through the Scholastic Teacher Store.

Visit the Scholastic Teacher Store ->

Quick Snapshot

What Kat Can Do

  • 45-minute talks that can be tailored to your schedule.
  • School, library, conference, and book club presentations.
  • Projected research images that connect books to science.
  • Interactive Q&A plus signing and conversation afterward.
  • Workshop and breakout session leadership for writing craft.

Best Fit

Topics Students Love

  • How story ideas begin.
  • How research shapes science fiction.
  • The path from idea to publication.
  • Deep-sea life, future tech, and strange science.
  • Writing adventure for tween and teen readers.

Direct Contact

Email Kat directly for school visits, conference invitations, Skype requests, and presentation questions.

katfalls77@gmail.com

In Person

School and Library Visits

Kat is comfortable speaking to groups of any size and can adapt her presentation for one class, a library audience, a full school assembly, or a conference setting.

Virtual

Free Skype Requests

For schools or readers who want a shorter online option, Skype visits are always free. Send a proposed date and time by email and include SKYPE REQUEST in the subject line.

Kat Falls

NYT bestselling author of Dark Life and Inhuman.
Evanston, IL.
